---
title: "Dominik Jud"
sortby: "Jud Dominik" # Note it is SURNAME first!
subtitle: "PhD student - Language Evolution and Population History"
image: dominik_jud.png # name this file as name_surname.png, same prefix as this file; this should be a square image 250px by 250px
toc: false
gscholar_id: "1l9QfeYAAAAJ" # replace this wiht your id and uncomment
group_start: "2025"
papers_name: "DC Jud" # if you have multiple names in papers, separated them by semicolon


#resources: 
#  - "NAME_SURNAME_cv.pdf" #optional, if you want to share your CV
about:
  id: person-profile
  template: jolla
  links:  # this are optional, use the ones that you are interested in (you need at least one, or you need to commone out this line as well)
    - icon: link
      text: NCCR Evolving Language
      href: https://evolvinglanguage.ch
#   - icon: file-earmark-text
#     text: vitae
#     href: ./people/NAME_SURNAME_cv.pdf
#    - icon: mortarboard
#      text: GScholar
#      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=YOUR_ID&hl=en
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/dominik-jud-8a4865303/
#    - icon: twitter
#      text: twitter
#      href: https://www.twitter.com/YOUR_ID
#    - text: "{{< fa brands bluesky >}} BlueSky"
#      href: https://bsky.app/profile/YOUR_ID
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/dominikjud
    - icon: link
      text: website
      href: https://dominikjud.github.io/web/index.html

---

:::{#person-profile}
:::


I am a PhD student based at the University of Zurich in the Human Evolutionary Ecology Group and a visiting student at the Evolutionary Ecology Group in the Zoology Department, University of Cambridge. My research focuses on how population history shapes language evolution, specifically considering how demographic processes such as population connectivity and population expansions have shaped the diversity of languages we see today.

I have a background in anthropology and worked on stress hormones in small-scale societies for my Master's.
